titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/250

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=231 status=live modified=2017-02-24 13:33:16.425651+00 | translation moa=230 status=live modified=2017-02-24 13:33:18.901534+00

div.p:
. W . dei Gratia Rex Scott’ : Omnibus probis Hominibus Tocius Terre Sue . Clericis
⁊ laicis  Salutem. Sciant presentes ⁊ futuri . me Concessisse . ⁊ Hac Carta mea
Confirmasse . Deo ⁊ Ecclesie Sancte crucis de Edenburg : ⁊ Canonicis ibidem deo
seruientibus ⁊ seruituris  Ecclesias illas in Galweia . quas fergus . ⁊ Vchtredus filius eius .
⁊ DauiD . filius Terri . eis dederunt. Quas Ecclesias . Iohannes Candide Case Episcopus eisdem
Canonicis Carta sua Confirmauit . ⁊ in proprios usus Conuertendas . concessit. Tenend’ . in liberam ⁊ puram ⁊ perpetuam Elemosinam . cum Omnibus iustis perti
nentiis suis . ita libere ⁊ Quiete . plenarie ⁊ Honorifice . sicut Carta predicti
Iohannis . Episcopi testatur. Testibus Waltero . Episcopo Glasguense . Hug’ . abbate de Iedd’ . Oliuer . Capel
lano meo . Philipp’ . de Valon’ . Camerario . meo . Willelmo . Cumin . Iustic’ . Scott’ : Willelmo de Bosch’ .
Clerico meo . Willelmo . de Valon’ . Ric’ . Reuel. Apud Ieddewrth . xviii . die Aprilis.

William by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, Greeting: Let present and future know that I have granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh and to the canons serving God there now and to come those churches in Galloway that Fergus and Uhtred his son and David son of Terrus gave to them. These churches John the bishop of Whithorn confirmed to the same canons by his charter, and granted, to be converted to their own benefit. To be held in free and pure and perpetual alms, with all their just pertinents, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, as the charter of the aforesaid Bishop John bears witness. As witnesses, Walter the bishop of Glasgow, Hugh the abbot of Jedburgh, Oliver my chaplain, Philip de Valognes my chamberlain, William Comyn, justice of the Scots, William del Bois my clerk, William de Valognes, Richard Revel. At Jedburgh on the 18th day of April.
